{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Specialist",
  "description": "Action Against Hunger is the world&rsquo;s hunger specialist and leader in a global movement that aims to end life-threatening hunger for good within our lifetimes. For 40 years, the humanitarian and development organization has been on the front lines, treating and preventing hunger across nearly 50 countries. It served more than 21 million people in 2018 alone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Specialist

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Job type: Full time
Category: Logistics
Reporting to: Deputy Head of Logistics Department
Proposed start date: June 2026

Description


	ACF Nigeria is currently looking to fill the role of Procurement Specialist who will support to ensure the implementation of the Procurement strategy and its coordination at country level.


Key Responsibilities
Mission 1 - Contribute to the definition of the country procurement strategy and ensure its implementation:


	Contribute to the strategic definition of the country procurement component;
	Monitor the implementation of the country procurement strategy via a detailed action plan;
	Anticipate program needs and plan the appropriate types of procedures to be developed;
	Optimize the number of procedures according to the markets.


Mission 2 - Coordinate and analyze the consultation of markets (Call for Interest, Call for Tender, etc.):


	Publish annually the Calls for Expression of Interest related to the markets targeted by ACF;
	Initiate the capital Call for Tender related to the needs of the country office;
	Conduct market analysis and supplier sourcing;
	Accompany the applicants in the development of their terms of reference and/or their technical specifications.


Mission 3 - Managing procurement at capital level:


	Implementation of and compliance with ACF procurement procedures;
	Manage the quality and optimization of procurement in the capital according to the objectives defined with the Logistics Head of Department and his deputy;
	Comply with donor-specific procurement rules where applicable;
	Anticipate and alert on risks related to the duration of procurement;
	Define procurement standards in accordance with the ACF framework;
	Develop a responsible procurement approach, in particular by identifying environmental criteria in our selection processes.


Mission 4 - Follow, maintain and develop the relationship with suppliers:


	Guarantee and develop supplier relations: sourcing, negotiation, contracting, follow-up;
	Regularly prospect the market in order to enrich the supplier database (via other NGOs, field visits, internet research, etc.);
	Conduct systematic supplier visits for all new businesses;
	Carry out supplier visits as part of the Call for Interest, Call for Tender, etc..
	Ensure that ACF policies (Gender and Safeguarding, etc.) are well understood by suppliers ;
	Participate in investigations in the event of a fraud alert or non-compliance with ACF policies by a supplier and propose corrective measures.


Mission 5 - Ensure the quality of goods and services according to the framework defined in the quality assurance:


	Ensure that the quality proposed by the suppliers meets the expectations of the applicant (in connection with the specifications, technical specifications);
	Document supplier visits via Visit Report that focus on the negotiation of the quality and service initially proposed;
	Ensure the management of disputes on a signed contract.


Mission 6 - Ensure compliance of procurement activities in accordance with ACF procedures:


	Ensure that employees comply with ACF&#39;s procurement policy, strategy, procedures and tools;
	Prevent and manage any non-conformity in the procurement process;
	Identify the risks of fraud and corruption on its activity and the implementation of preventive and control actions;
	Raise employee awareness of the risks of fraud and corruption;
	Implement and follow up on recommendations following controls and audits.


Additional Responsibilities:


	This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive, and the employee will also perform other related tasks as required and be responsible for reporting and communicating progress and achievement of the specific assigned task.
	Maintenance of high technical standards.
	Professionally conduct all duties following ACF Nigeria mission staff regulations, ACF mandate and charter, including promotion of gender equality.


Position Requirements


	At least a Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Management, Logistics, Procurement or other related field.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel).
	Solid knowledge and understanding of procurement processes, policy and systems.
	Three (3) years of previous experience as a procurement officer or related position.
	Ability to work well with management and staff at all levels, multitask, prioritize, and manage time efficiently.
	Rigorous, with strong organization skills and attention to detail.
	Strong communication and negotiation skills.
	Previous experience working for NGOs an asset, particularly international and/or health related NGOs.
	Fluency in one or more national/regional languages an asset.


Note


	At Action Against Hunger, we foster a work culture that champions diversity, equity and inclusion.&nbsp; We are committed to creating an environment where every employee feels valued, respected and empowered to thrive
	Action Against Hunger does not charge any fee at any stage of the recruitment process &ndash; this includes application, interview, or processing fees.
	All applicants MUST answer every question in the application form. Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Procurement Specialist Job at Action Against Hunger




        
    

        
        Action Against Hunger is the world’s hunger specialist and leader in a global movement that aims to end life-threatening hunger for good within our lifetimes. For 40 years, the humanitarian and development organization has been on the front lines, treating and preventing hunger across nearly 50 countries. It served more than 21 million people in 2018 alone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Specialist

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Job type: Full time
Category: Logistics
Reporting to: Deputy Head of Logistics Department
Proposed start date: June 2026

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	ACF Nigeria is currently looking to fill the role of Procurement Specialist who will support to ensure the implementation of the Procurement strategy and its coordination at country level.


Key Responsibilities
Mission 1 - Contribute to the definition of the country procurement strategy and ensure its implementation:


	Contribute to the strategic definition of the country procurement component;
	Monitor the implementation of the country procurement strategy via a detailed action plan;
	Anticipate program needs and plan the appropriate types of procedures to be developed;
	Optimize the number of procedures according to the markets.


Mission 2 - Coordinate and analyze the consultation of markets (Call for Interest, Call for Tender, etc.):


	Publish annually the Calls for Expression of Interest related to the markets targeted by ACF;
	Initiate the capital Call for Tender related to the needs of the country office;
	Conduct market analysis and supplier sourcing;
	Accompany the applicants in the development of their terms of reference and/or their technical specifications.


Mission 3 - Managing procurement at capital level:


	Implementation of and compliance with ACF procurement procedures;
	Manage the quality and optimization of procurement in the capital according to the objectives defined with the Logistics Head of Department and his deputy;
	Comply with donor-specific procurement rules where applicable;
	Anticipate and alert on risks related to the duration of procurement;
	Define procurement standards in accordance with the ACF framework;
	Develop a responsible procurement approach, in particular by identifying environmental criteria in our selection processes.


Mission 4 - Follow, maintain and develop the relationship with suppliers:


	Guarantee and develop supplier relations: sourcing, negotiation, contracting, follow-up;
	Regularly prospect the market in order to enrich the supplier database (via other NGOs, field visits, internet research, etc.);
	Conduct systematic supplier visits for all new businesses;
	Carry out supplier visits as part of the Call for Interest, Call for Tender, etc..
	Ensure that ACF policies (Gender and Safeguarding, etc.) are well understood by suppliers ;
	Participate in investigations in the event of a fraud alert or non-compliance with ACF policies by a supplier and propose corrective measures.


Mission 5 - Ensure the quality of goods and services according to the framework defined in the quality assurance:


	Ensure that the quality proposed by the suppliers meets the expectations of the applicant (in connection with the specifications, technical specifications);
	Document supplier visits via Visit Report that focus on the negotiation of the quality and service initially proposed;
	Ensure the management of disputes on a signed contract.


Mission 6 - Ensure compliance of procurement activities in accordance with ACF procedures:


	Ensure that employees comply with ACF's procurement policy, strategy, procedures and tools;
	Prevent and manage any non-conformity in the procurement process;
	Identify the risks of fraud and corruption on its activity and the implementation of preventive and control actions;
	Raise employee awareness of the risks of fraud and corruption;
	Implement and follow up on recommendations following controls and audits.


Additional Responsibilities:


	This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive, and the employee will also perform other related tasks as required and be responsible for reporting and communicating progress and achievement of the specific assigned task.
	Maintenance of high technical standards.
	Professionally conduct all duties following ACF Nigeria mission staff regulations, ACF mandate and charter, including promotion of gender equality.


Position Requirements


	At least a Bachelor’s Degree in Business Management, Logistics, Procurement or other related field.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel).
	Solid knowledge and understanding of procurement processes, policy and systems.
	Three (3) years of previous experience as a procurement officer or related position.
	Ability to work well with management and staff at all levels, multitask, prioritize, and manage time efficiently.
	Rigorous, with strong organization skills and attention to detail.
	Strong communication and negotiation skills.
	Previous experience working for NGOs an asset, particularly international and/or health related NGOs.
	Fluency in one or more national/regional languages an asset.


Note


	At Action Against Hunger, we foster a work culture that champions diversity, equity and inclusion.  We are committed to creating an environment where every employee feels valued, respected and empowered to thrive
	Action Against Hunger does not charge any fee at any stage of the recruitment process – this includes application, interview, or processing fees.
	All applicants MUST answer every question in the application form. Incomplete applications will not be considered.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Alpha-Beta Consulting Limited
content:
Alpha-Beta Consulting Limited is a leading Nigerian firm offering expert Business and IT solutions. For over two decades, the firm has partnered with financial institutions and public sector organisations across Nigeria and Sub-Saharan Africa, earning a strong reputation for excellence in revenue assurance and digital transformation. The firm provides independent, professional advice in Information Technology and Financial Systems, focused on enhancing client performance and operational efficiency. Its approach blends cutting-edge technologies with client-centric strategies to drive long-term value.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Led by a team of seasoned professionals in IT consulting, banking, and accounting, Alpha-Beta delivers tailored solutions grounded in deep industry insight. Its commitment to excellence is underpinned by a focus on recruiting high-performing professionals who align with the firm’s full-service delivery model. Strategic partnerships with global software developers and technical experts allow the firm to offer scalable and innovative services. These alliances ensure access to advanced tools, specialised training, and strategic guidance.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Analyst, Database Administration

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Database Administration

Job Summary


	The Analyst, Database Administrator role provides hands?on exposure to database administration, data analysis, and reporting within a live production environment.
	The role supports the build, monitoring, integrity, and security of organisational databases while assisting with data quality management, trend analysis, and reporting to support business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in writing and optimizing SQL queries for data extraction, transformation, and analysis
	Support database backup and recovery processes
	Create, update, and maintain data reports, dashboards, and analytical tools
	Perform data analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and insights
	Collaborate with business users to translate data needs into actionable reports
	Support the development of business intelligence reports and visualizations
	Assist in maintaining data integrity, accuracy, and consistency
	Participate in improving existing reporting processes and systems
	Support data cleaning, validation, and quality assurance processes
	Document data analysis procedures, reports, and workflows
	Generate ad?hoc reports for business units as required
	Attend team meetings and support project and operational activities
	Comply with data protection, governance, and internal IT policies


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Accuracy and timeliness of reports and dashboards
	Successful execution of SQL queries and data requests
	Data integrity and consistency maintained
	Turnaround time for resolving data?related issues
	Quality of documentation and reporting processes
	Adherence to data governance and security standards
	Database uptime and reliability
	Effectiveness in translating business requirements into insights


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology, Data Science, or related field
	2-4 years of relevant functional experience
	Basic knowledge of relational databases and SQL (e.g., SELECT, JOIN, INSERT)
	Strong interest in data analysis and database administration
	Familiarity with Microsoft SQL Server or similar databases is an advantage


Performance Expectations:


	Timely and accurate delivery of assigned data analysis tasks
	Quality and accuracy of reports, dashboards, and query outputs
	Ability to identify data trends, inconsistencies, and anomalies
	Effective collaboration with technical and business teams
	Continuous improvement in SQL, reporting, and analytical skills


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem?solving skills
	Close attention to detail and logical thinking
	Willingness to learn and accept feedback
	Good written and verbal communication skills
	Ability to meet deadlines and follow instructions
	Team?oriented mindset
	Understanding of data confidentiality and security principles


Work Environment:


	Supportive, collaborative, and learning?focused team culture
	Exposure to real?time data analysis environments
	Office?based or hybrid work arrangement (subject to business needs)
	Access to internal training and development resources


Key Relationships:


	Internal: Database Analysts, Developers, Project Managers, Business Intelligence, IT Operations
	External: None (unless assigned under supervision).


Salary
N350,000 - N420,000 per month. 

Application Closing Date: 21st May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Analyst, Database Administrator” as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Senior Specialist – Information Systems (IS)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Are you a seasoned Information Systems professional with strong technical depth, leadership capability, and a passion for driving innovation?
	We are looking for a Senior Specialist – Information Systems to support and strengthen our IT infrastructure, systems strategy, and operational excellence.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the IS Manager in overseeing IT systems, network infrastructure, and cloud environments
	Lead system design, deployment, upgrades, and maintenance across platforms
	Drive infrastructure reliability, cybersecurity, and business continuity initiatives
	Provide technical leadership, mentoring, and supervision to IS team members
	Manage vendor relationships, procurement processes, and system inventory controls
	Implement DevSecOps practices, automation, and emerging technology solutions
	Ensure compliance with data protection regulations (NDPR, GDPR)


Success Metrics


	High system uptime (cloud & on-premise)
	Strong cybersecurity ratings and risk management outcomes
	Effective backup, recovery, and business continuity systems
	Successful delivery of IS projects within defined constraints
	Continuous improvement and innovation (AI, automation, emerging tech)


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
	9–12 years of experience in Information Systems design & administration
	Minimum of 3 years in a leadership role
	Strong expertise in cloud platforms (Azure), networking, DevOps, and infrastructure
	Hands-on experience with technologies such as M365/O365, Active Directory, Docker, Kubernetes, APIs, and virtualisation


Certifications (Advantageous):


	ITIL® 4
	Microsoft Azure Administrator (AZ-104)
	CISSP
	Cisco Certified Internetwork Expert (CCIE)


What We’re Looking For:


	Strong leadership, analytical, and problem-solving skills
	close attention to detail and organisational capability
	Ability to work independently and under pressure
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
	Integrity, accountability, and professionalism.


Salary
N500,000 - N650,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 21st May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Senior Specialist – Information Systems (IS)" as the subject of the email.

 

3.) Digital Marketing Manager

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	In this role, you will be responsible for designing and executing high-impact digital campaigns that resonate with our audience and convert leads into long-term partners.
	We are looking for someone who stays ahead of digital trends and knows how to balance data-driven insights with compelling storytelling.


Key Responsibilities


	Strategy & Execution: Develop and implement end-to-end digital marketing strategies to increase brand awareness and lead generation.
	Social Media Management: Oversee all social media accounts, creating engaging content calendars and managing daily interactions.
	Content Creation: Produce high-quality written and visual content for blogs, newsletters, and social platforms.
	Paid Advertising: Set up and optimize PPC campaigns (Google Ads, Meta Ads) to ensure maximum ROI.
	SEO & Analytics: Monitor website traffic and SEO performance, providing monthly reports on KPIs and actionable growth insights.
	Email Marketing: Design and manage email automation workflows to nurture our lead database.


Requirements


	Experience: 3+ years of proven experience in digital marketing, preferably within the corporate or financial services sector.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency with Google Analytics, Meta Business Suite, Canva (or Adobe Suite), and email marketing tools (e.g., Mailchimp).
	Copywriting: Exceptional writing skills with the ability to adopt the brand’s professional yet engaging voice.
	Analytical Mindset: Comfortable working with data to justify marketing spend and strategy shifts.
	Creativity: A "growth-hacker" mindset—constantly looking for new ways to reach our target demographic.


What We Offer


	Salary: N200,000 - N250,000 / Month.
	Competitive salary based on experience.
	A collaborative environment where your ideas are heard and implemented.
	Opportunity to work within a diversified and growing group of companies.


Application Closing Date: 24th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualfied candidates should send their CV and a portfolio of your previous campaigns or creative work to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Management Advisor - Industrial Energy Technology",
  "description": "Baker Hughes, a GE company (NYSE:BHGE), is the world&#39;s first and only fullstream provider of integrated oilfield products, services and digital solutions. Drawing on a storied heritage of invention, BHGE harnesses the passion and experience of its people to enhance productivity across the oil and gas value chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Management Advisor - Industrial Energy Technology

Job Requisition ID: R162723
Job Location: Nigeria
Job type: Full time

Description
Join our cutting-edge Technology Team:


	Our Team offers a plant-wide, holistic suite of computerized machine condition monitoring and protection services.
	We help our customers and partners to achieve outstanding levels of reliability through our innovative technical designs and platforms.
	Our team provides vibration monitoring, machinery protection, diagnostics, real-time data, and remote monitoring services to our customers.


Work with the latest innovations:


	As a Project Manager, you will be responsible for the project delivery of all Turbo-Machinery upgrades-related projects with profit &amp; loss accountability.
	The Project Manager is also responsible for driving customer satisfaction, assuring the highest excellence in the execution jointly to the business targets


Responsibilities
As a Project Manager, you will be responsible for:


	Managing overall execution of rotating machinery equipment upgrades/retrofit related projects and accountable for the financial performance of the contract in terms of revenues, contribution margin and cash collection.
	Managing the scope of work of the contract and ensuring that the quality and on time delivery of the project are achieved to meet Customer needs and satisfaction.
	Managing and coordinate all related Rotating Machinery equipment unit modification and site installation activities, Unit Upgrade &amp; Repair Service and all parts delivery activities to meet Contract obligation
	Managing cross-functional teams and driving both internal and external execution to meet contractual obligations and business targets.
	Organizing and, conducting all contract review meetings and leading all forms of communication with customers, managing project deliverable and discussing/reviewing outcomes also with all internal stakeholders including the Project Management Leader
	Identifying and carefully evaluating risks during the project lifecycle and working with multiple functions to mitigate them
	Managing and Negotiating all change orders with Customers
	Planning and coordinating with Customers, the implementation of recommended actions due to Technical Information Letters by monitoring their technical improvement applicability and execution timeframe on all related Rotating Machinery equipment
	Resolving with the customer any un-clarity in the contract agreement wording which may arise during the execution of the contract
	Keeping Customers updated about design changes and provide information about the latest advancements in upgrades opportunities to Customer, thereby sourcing for opportunities to expand the project profitability in ways that bring value to Customers.
	Operating according to performance indicators, such as timeliness of project launch and quality of project report, customer satisfaction NPS.
	Interacting compliantly with Baker Hughes tools, processes, and organization in order to fulfill all Contract obligations
	Prioritizing and promptly escalate critical issues business needs


Requirements
To be successful in this role you will:


	Have a Bachelor&#39;s Degree in engineering from an accredited College or University with Oil &amp; Gas industry experience
	Have at least 5 years of experience in project management that includes rotating machinery equipment upgrades/retrofit
	Have excellent customer handling experience
	Experience on site supervision with direct practice on the field managing people and resources.
	Demonstrate technical writing skills, knowledge of MS Office and systems for design, test and analysis (e.g. AutoCad)
	Demonstrate the ability to manage and execute multiple customer projects to successful completion
	Show experience of project cost control and managing customer contractual requirements to successful completion
	Demonstrate excellent communication skills and an ability to build trusting relationships with customers
	Be able to work in a variety of difficult environments including on-shore facilities and customer locations
	Demonstrate ability to operate in a globally spread matrix organization
	Be IPMA or PMP certified
	Have Six Sigma Green Belt certified is a plus
	Ability to travel (30%)
",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-16",

Project Management Advisor - Industrial Energy Technology Job at Baker Hughes




        
    

        
        Baker Hughes, a GE company (NYSE:BHGE), is the world's first and only fullstream provider of integrated oilfield products, services and digital solutions. Drawing on a storied heritage of invention, BHGE harnesses the passion and experience of its people to enhance productivity across the oil and gas value chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Management Advisor - Industrial Energy Technology

Job Requisition ID: R162723
Job Location: Nigeria
Job type: Full time

Description
Join our cutting-edge Technology Team:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our Team offers a plant-wide, holistic suite of computerized machine condition monitoring and protection services.
	We help our customers and partners to achieve outstanding levels of reliability through our innovative technical designs and platforms.
	Our team provides vibration monitoring, machinery protection, diagnostics, real-time data, and remote monitoring services to our customers.


Work with the latest innovations:


	As a Project Manager, you will be responsible for the project delivery of all Turbo-Machinery upgrades-related projects with profit & loss accountability.
	The Project Manager is also responsible for driving customer satisfaction, assuring the highest excellence in the execution jointly to the business targets


Responsibilities
As a Project Manager, you will be responsible for:


	Managing overall execution of rotating machinery equipment upgrades/retrofit related projects and accountable for the financial performance of the contract in terms of revenues, contribution margin and cash collection.
	Managing the scope of work of the contract and ensuring that the quality and on time delivery of the project are achieved to meet Customer needs and satisfaction.
	Managing and coordinate all related Rotating Machinery equipment unit modification and site installation activities, Unit Upgrade & Repair Service and all parts delivery activities to meet Contract obligation
	Managing cross-functional teams and driving both internal and external execution to meet contractual obligations and business targets.
	Organizing and, conducting all contract review meetings and leading all forms of communication with customers, managing project deliverable and discussing/reviewing outcomes also with all internal stakeholders including the Project Management Leader
	Identifying and carefully evaluating risks during the project lifecycle and working with multiple functions to mitigate them
	Managing and Negotiating all change orders with Customers
	Planning and coordinating with Customers, the implementation of recommended actions due to Technical Information Letters by monitoring their technical improvement applicability and execution timeframe on all related Rotating Machinery equipment
	Resolving with the customer any un-clarity in the contract agreement wording which may arise during the execution of the contract
	Keeping Customers updated about design changes and provide information about the latest advancements in upgrades opportunities to Customer, thereby sourcing for opportunities to expand the project profitability in ways that bring value to Customers.
	Operating according to performance indicators, such as timeliness of project launch and quality of project report, customer satisfaction NPS.
	Interacting compliantly with Baker Hughes tools, processes, and organization in order to fulfill all Contract obligations
	Prioritizing and promptly escalate critical issues business needs


Requirements
To be successful in this role you will:


	Have a Bachelor's Degree in engineering from an accredited College or University with Oil & Gas industry experience
	Have at least 5 years of experience in project management that includes rotating machinery equipment upgrades/retrofit
	Have excellent customer handling experience
	Experience on site supervision with direct practice on the field managing people and resources.
	Demonstrate technical writing skills, knowledge of MS Office and systems for design, test and analysis (e.g. AutoCad)
	Demonstrate the ability to manage and execute multiple customer projects to successful completion
	Show experience of project cost control and managing customer contractual requirements to successful completion
	Demonstrate excellent communication skills and an ability to build trusting relationships with customers
	Be able to work in a variety of difficult environments including on-shore facilities and customer locations
	Demonstrate ability to operate in a globally spread matrix organization
	Be IPMA or PMP certified
	Have Six Sigma Green Belt certified is a plus
	Ability to travel (30%)
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "Vivaflowers.ng is a dynamic floral company specializing in delivering fresh, beautifully arranged flowers across Nigeria. We are committed to providing our customers with top-notch service and quality products.&nbsp;

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer
Job Location: Jahi, Abuja (FCT)
Work Mode: On-site (Full Time)
Working Days: Monday to Sunday with 2 days off weekly

Job Summary


	We are seeking a smart, organized, and proactive Administrative Officer to support daily office operations and ensure smooth coordination within the company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling administrative tasks, maintaining records, assisting with customer communication, and supporting the team in achieving operational efficiency.
	Strong communication skills, attention to detail, professionalism, and the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment are essential for this role.


Key Responsibilities


	Arrange fresh flowers into bouquets and luxury floral designs.
	Assist with flower wrapping, packaging, and presentation.
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the workspace.
	Handle flowers carefully to ensure freshness and quality.
	Assist with customer orders and ensure timely preparation.
	Work with the team to create visually appealing floral displays.
	Support daily store operations when needed.


Requirements


	HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree, or related qualifications.
	Prior florist experience is not required.
	Basic knowledge of flowers or floral arrangement is an added advantage.
	Creative, neat, and detail-oriented.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.
	Male candidates are encouraged to apply.


Benefits


	Salary: N100,000 monthly
	Opportunity for growth within a thriving company.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: Vivaflowers.ng@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Administrative Officer Job at Vivaflowers.ng




        
    

        
        Vivaflowers.ng is a dynamic floral company specializing in delivering fresh, beautifully arranged flowers across Nigeria. We are committed to providing our customers with top-notch service and quality products. 

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer
Job Location: Jahi, Abuja (FCT)
Work Mode: On-site (Full Time)
Working Days: Monday to Sunday with 2 days off weekly

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a smart, organized, and proactive Administrative Officer to support daily office operations and ensure smooth coordination within the company.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for handling administrative tasks, maintaining records, assisting with customer communication, and supporting the team in achieving operational efficiency.
	Strong communication skills, attention to detail, professionalism, and the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment are essential for this role.


Key Responsibilities


	Arrange fresh flowers into bouquets and luxury floral designs.
	Assist with flower wrapping, packaging, and presentation.
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the workspace.
	Handle flowers carefully to ensure freshness and quality.
	Assist with customer orders and ensure timely preparation.
	Work with the team to create visually appealing floral displays.
	Support daily store operations when needed.


Requirements


	HND / Bachelor’s Degree, or related qualifications.
	Prior florist experience is not required.
	Basic knowledge of flowers or floral arrangement is an added advantage.
	Creative, neat, and detail-oriented.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
	Good communication and teamwork skills.
	Male candidates are encouraged to apply.


Benefits


	Salary: N100,000 monthly
	Opportunity for growth within a thriving company.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Virtual Assistant Job at Respected Solutions and Investment Limited




        
    

        
        Respected Solutions and Investment Limited is a government approved short term loan and pawn shop in Lagos that is committed to giving fast, safe, and convenient loans against luxury items. We have years of experience and a reputation for transparency, dependability, and professionalism. We give fair market values and flexible repayment terms for an instant cash loan or a gold loan, watch loan, or jewelry loan. Our professional team guarantees that your property is treated with care and safely stored until you repay your loan in full. If you're looking for pawn shops close to you, we are your trusted partner, dedicated to helping you realize the cash value of your property without selling it.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Title: Virtual Assistant

Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	Are you a night owl with exceptional communication skills? Respected Solution is looking for a dedicated and reliable Virtual Assistant to manage our after-hours operations. This role is critical in ensuring our clients and followers receive seamless support while our physical offices are closed.
	As our Virtual Assistant, you will be the "digital face" of the company during the night, handling all incoming inquiries via our official phone lines and social media platforms.


Key Responsibilities


	Call Management: Handle all incoming calls on the office phone line professionally, providing information or escalating urgent issues as needed.
	Social Media Engagement: Monitor and respond promptly to messages, comments, and inquiries across all social media platforms (Instagram, WhatsApp, Facebook, etc.).
	Inquiry Resolution: Provide accurate information regarding our services, following company guidelines to ensure high-quality customer service.
	Daily Reporting: Maintain a log of all interactions and provide a brief handover report at the end of the shift (6:00 AM).


Requirements


	Availability: Must be able to work the 7:00 PM to 6:00 AM shift consistently.
	Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills (fluency in English is essential).
	Tech Savvy: Comfortable using social media management tools, VoIP/office phone systems, and messaging apps.
	Environment: Must have a quiet workspace, a reliable high-speed internet connection, and a stable power supply (Inverter/Generator backup).
	Reliability: High level of discipline to work independently without direct supervision during night hours.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 per month. 

What We Offer:


	Competitive monthly salary.
	The flexibility of working from the comfort of your home.
	A chance to be part of a structured and growing corporate group.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a short note explaining your experience with remote customer service to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	If you are organized, proactive, and ready to take on the night shift, we want to hear from you!
	Respected Solution is an equal-opportunity employer. We value professionalism and dedication above all else.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
